fix: accessibility — ARIA labels, live region, keyboard, contrast, motion

- ARIA labels: Add aria-label to mic, stop, send, upload, new chat buttons
- ARIA live region: transcript div gets role="log" aria-live="polite" so
  screen readers announce new messages
- Keyboard shortcut: Ctrl+Shift+Space triggers push-to-talk from anywhere
- Thinking toggle: Add role="button", tabindex="0", aria-expanded, and
  keydown handler (Enter/Space) for keyboard-only users
- Color contrast: Bump --text-muted from #484f58 to #6e7681 (~4:1 ratio
  on #0d1117 background, up from ~2.6:1)
- Reduced motion: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) disables mic
  pulse animation, canvas/status transitions, thinking collapse animation,
  and streaming dots for users who prefer less motion

commit.message:
Fix overly aggressive blocked page detection killing Brightdata results

isBlockedPage was matching "403 forbidden" and "access denied" on ANY
page regardless of size — these strings appear in legitimate product
page content. Now only flags these on very short pages (<2KB) that are
clearly challenge/block pages, not real PDPs. Also adds server-side
logging to see Brightdata response sizes.

Phase 2: Integration catalog — CRUD API + frontend pages

Backend: Pydantic schemas for Integration/Endpoint CRUD, FastAPI router
with list/get/create/update/delete for integrations and nested endpoints,
seed script with Salesforce, Zendesk, and Twilio sample data.

Frontend: React Query hooks for integration API, IntegrationList page
with card grid, IntegrationDetail with endpoints table, IntegrationForm
for create/edit, and updated routing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Tune ESP-NOW coex for reliable sync under BLE load

Per-retry delivery was ~9% with default coex settings, causing ~40%
broadcast failure rate even with 5 retries. Fix: prioritise WiFi
(ESP_COEX_PREFER_WIFI), disable WiFi power save, max TX power
(19.5 dBm), and increase retries from 5 to 10 over ~1.5s window.
Test suite now passes 10/10 with avg sync latency ~130ms.

commit.message:
fix: use Qt Wayland plugin instead of xcb for Calamares

The xcb plugin has a broken dependency chain on NixOS (missing
libxcb-cursor in RPATH). Instead of fighting with LD_LIBRARY_PATH,
add qt6.qtwayland via QT_PLUGIN_PATH and run Calamares natively
on Wayland. Pass WAYLAND_DISPLAY and XDG_RUNTIME_DIR to root
process so it can connect to the compositor.

Also enable QT_DEBUG_PLUGINS=1 for diagnostic output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Improve WASM loading speed (#560)

Improve WASM loading speed with compression, caching, and LTO

- Add gzip/brotli compression middleware (CompressionLayer) — the 11 MB
  WASM binary compresses to ~2-3 MB over the wire
- Add Cache-Control headers: hashed assets get 1-year immutable cache,
  index.html gets no-cache for revalidation
- Add release profile: LTO, single codegen unit, opt-level "z", strip
  debug info for smaller binaries

Co-authored-by: MattyG <meawoppl@cfl-test-bench.localdomain>

commit.message:
Fix config editor not showing disabled apps

Store the full merged config (including disabled apps) on TidbytDisplay._raw_config.
The /api/config/raw GET endpoint now returns this directly instead of reconstructing
from instantiated app objects, which excluded any app with enabled:false.

Also consolidate default app config into _default_apps_config() so new apps
automatically appear in the editor even when loading an older config file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(matlot): add Pilne urgent view toggle button

- Backend: PILNE status filter returns pending rows that are past-due
  OR new today; urgent_count computed before status filter so button
  stays visible from any tab (Zatwierdzone, Wszystkie, etc.)
- HTML: 'Pilne' cat-btn with red active state, hidden by default
- JS: setStatus handles PILNE; updatePilneButton shows/hides button
  and auto-drops back to N view when urgent_count reaches 0;
  bulk release button enabled in PILNE view (no filter required)

commit.message:
Full legibility sweep: eliminate all sub-11px text across entire deck

- Rebuilt Multi-Persona slide (s12) with clamp() responsive sizing
  - Role names: 13px → clamp(14px,1.3vw,18px)
  - Descriptions: 10px → clamp(12px,1.1vw,15px)
  - Checklist items: 10px → clamp(12px,1.1vw,15px)
  - Icons: 14px → 16px, icon boxes: 28px → 32px
  - Bottom row (Athlete, Fan): 9-12px → clamp(12-14px)
  - Total Roles number: 22px → clamp(24px,2.8vw,36px)
- Global sweep across all 29 slides (386 instances fixed):
  - 7px (1 instance) → 10px
  - 8px (115 instances) → 11px
  - 9px (127 instances) → 12px
  - 10px (120 instances) → 12px
- Cover slide stat numbers: 18px → clamp(18px,2.2vw,26px)
- Zero instances of sub-11px inline font sizes remain

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix hunger-full guard with tolerance for near-max values

Hunger displayed as 100/100 could be 99.5+ due to float precision or
partial restoration. Use 0.5 tolerance so items that would restore
effectively zero are blocked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: clean up GCP resources on failed terraform apply (#550)

When terraform apply fails mid-flight, the deferred cleanup was deleting
the workDir (including terraform.tfstate) without first running terraform
destroy. This left orphaned GCP resources (e.g. in-flight instance
creation operations) that count against the concurrent operations quota
and are never reclaimed.

Now tracks whether terraform apply was attempted and runs terraform
destroy before removing the workDir, ensuring partial resources are
cleaned up.

Co-authored-by: Andy Wolf <andy.wolf@medaware.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
